---
title: "About"
pagetitle: "About · Arpan Acharya"
---

## What I'm working on

I'm currently a **consultant at the Nepal Health Economics Association (NHEA)**,
where I work with health-sector data and contribute to applied economic
research — currently on the **allocative and technical efficiency of Nepal's
health sector**.

Alongside this, I'm pursuing a couple of independent research projects: one on
**gender norms, fertility, and female labour force participation** in Nepal,
and some exploratory work on the **impact of conflict** and **social mobility**
in the Nepali context.

I also love teaching. I spend part of my time teaching **econometrics** and
**causal inference**, and I take on occasional freelance research-support work
for graduate students and professionals.

## Research interests

My primary fields are:

- **Causal inference** and applied econometrics
- **Development economics**
- **Political economy** of conflict, institutions, and inclusion
- **Health economics**

## Beyond economics

I'm broadly curious about how **institutions, identity, and history** shape
economic life and I read across anthropology, political science, and history to inform my empirical work. I'm drawn to questions where the most interesting variation comes from *social dynamics, political representation and systems of governance, and how these factors shape individual and collective responses to policy.*

## Data and tools

I work mostly in **R**, **Python**, and **Stata**. I've also started folding **AI coding agents** into my
day-to-day workflow. A lot of my research involves **cleaning and scraping
data** sometimes as part of a job, sometimes for independent projects. And
some of that code is up on my [GitHub](https://github.com/acharyaarpan).

## Get in touch

The easiest way to reach me is by [email](mailto:arpanacharya11.aa@gmail.com).
You can also find me on [GitHub](https://github.com/acharyaarpan) and
[LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/arpan1acharya/).
